title: Facing Pressure, Trump Officials Reject Claims They’re Softening on Immigration
summary: Immigration hard-liners have grown frustrated with the level of deportations and the Department of Homeland Security’s attempts, under its new secretary, to stay under the radar.

title: Mahmoud Khalil Hurtles Toward Potential Deportation as U.S. Speeds Case
summary: A key judicial decision in Mr. Khalil’s immigration case was expedited significantly and included the recusal of multiple judges.

title: Trump Administration Wants to Strip 12 Immigrants of U.S. Citizenship
summary: The targeted Americans are accused of misdeeds that can qualify them to lose citizenship, but denaturalization has rarely been invoked in the past.

title: Lawmakers May Continue to Inspect ICE Detention Centers, Appeals Court Rules
summary: A federal appeals court declined, for now, to halt an order requiring D.H.S. to permit unannounced visits to immigration detention centers by Democrats in Congress.

title: Trump Plans to Fire F.D.A. Commissioner Marty Makary
summary: Dr. Makary has been a supporter of the Make American Healthy Again Movement but made some enemies in the administration over vaping, the abortion pill and rejections of new drugs.

title: U.F.O. Files Released by U.S. Shed Light on What the Government Knows
summary: The Pentagon on Friday released online what it called “new, never-before-seen” files, dating back decades, related to unidentified flying objects.

title: U.S. Conducts Third Boat Strike in 5 Days, Killing 2 and Leaving a Survivor
summary: Of the 57 attacks in the Caribbean and eastern Pacific against boats accused of drug trafficking, there have rarely been survivors.

title: Trump Gave Out a No-Bid Contract to Turn D.C.’s Reflecting Pool Blue
summary: President Trump handpicked a firm he said had worked on his swimming pool to repair the iconic site near the Lincoln Memorial.

title: Republicans Are Building an Advantage in Redistricting. How Much?
summary: Where things stands in the race for House control after recent court rulings.

title: Virginia Court Strikes Down Redistricted Voting Map in a Huge Blow to Democrats
summary: The decision is a major victory for Republicans, wiping away a measure approved by voters to allow Democrats to gain as many as four House seats in the midterms.

title: 4 Takeaways From the Virginia Supreme Court Gerrymandering Decision
summary: The ruling was a major lift to the G.O.P. efforts to build a structural advantage through redistricting.

title: Alabama Asks Supreme Court to Allow it to Use New Voting Map
summary: State officials urged the justices to allow them to jettison Alabama’s congressional district map, citing the Supreme Court’s recent decision that dealt a blow to the Voting Rights Act.

title: David Attenborough, a Voice of Nature, Turns 100
summary: Pictures and striking scenes from the making of perhaps the world’s most celebrated naturalist.

title: How China’s Leader Lost Faith in His Generals
summary: Xi Jinping spent 13 years building a military to rival that of the United States. But the stronger the Chinese forces grew, the less he trusted the generals he had handpicked to run them.

title: Why the Trump-Xi Summit Matters, Even if Little Seems to Come of It
summary: The war in Iran, trade, artificial intelligence and Taiwan are expected to be on the agenda. But expectations are modest.

title: China’s Exports and Imports Set Records in April Amid High Energy Costs
summary: The country’s exports surged and its trade surplus with the United States widened ahead of President Trump’s scheduled visit next week to Beijing.

title: A Less Muscular Victory Day Parade Shows Putin’s Growing Vulnerability
summary: The event, shrunken because of security fears, adds to a sense that Moscow and other cities can no longer be insulated from the war.

title: U.S. Targets Iran’s Missile and Drone Program With Sanctions
summary: The new measures are focused on Chinese companies that supply Iran’s military with materials to make drones.

summary: The slick, captured in satellite images near Kharg Island in the Persian Gulf, has raised concerns about the condition of Iran’s oil infrastructure.

summary: Former passengers on the MV Hondius say that the ship was made for wildlife expeditions and that the crew took safety very seriously.

title: Norovirus Outbreak Sickens 115 on Cruise Ship
summary: More than 100 passengers and 13 crew members on the Caribbean Princess reported being ill, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

title: Compare the Purported Epstein Suicide Note to His Writings
summary: The New York Times has not authenticated the note. But some of its phrases are similar or identical to ones Jeffrey Epstein used in emails and another handwritten letter.

title: In Kristin Smart Case, Soil Suggests Human Remains Once Present in Yard
summary: A California sheriff said investigators were searching the backyard of a woman whose son was convicted of murdering Ms. Smart, who went missing in 1996.

summary: End-of-school year chaos has arrived. These 20-minute (or less) meals will get you cooking through it.

title: Takeaways From the 2026 U.K. Local Elections
summary: Results from municipal and regional elections signaled major gains for the right-wing populist Reform U.K. and steep losses for Labour.

title: Blake Lively Settlement Had No Financial Payout, Filing Confirms
summary: Justin Baldoni’s company did not agree to make a payout as part of the deal that ended Ms. Lively’s lawsuit, but she is able to seek compensation through a separate legal avenue.

title: Proposed Rule by Postal Service Could Allow Handguns to Be Mailed
summary: The proposal comes after the Justice Department said this year that a century-old law banning the practice was unconstitutional. Democratic leaders are calling it “unlawful.”
